comment: comp-036 Monte Carlo + decision-rule parameters. Builds on comp-033 single-dose distributions; adds repeat-dose accumulation math and receptor-occupancy calculation.
comment_on_n_samples: 10,000 per regimen-and-dose-count combination. The brief specifies >=10,000 samples. With 24 regimen-cells (8 QD + 8 BID + 8 loading-plus-maintenance) and shared single-dose sampling, total simulation effort is bounded.

time_grid.comment: 0.5h time step over 7 days. 336 time points. Sufficient for capturing 4h-half-life IL-1Ra trough kinetics.

comment_on_flare_window: Acute gout flare peak inflammation is 24-72h; 0-72h captures the full window where IL-1 blockade is therapeutically meaningful.
comment_on_thresholds: Primary GREEN gate: median sustained occupancy >=80% over flare window (per brief)
Anakinra-comparator benchmark: ~85-90% mean occupancy at steady-state QD
Lower-bound: p25 occupancy >=60% — protects against catastrophic regimen failure in lower-prior-tail Monte Carlo iterations

decision_rule.GREEN_overall: At least one of three regimens (QD/BID/Loading+Maint) achieves median 80%+ sustained occupancy over 0-72h flare window AND total dose count <=14 days AND p25 occupancy at the recommended regimen >=60%
decision_rule.YELLOW_overall: Median 80%+ achievable but only with >14 days or >4 doses/day OR p25 stays in 40-60% range OR confidence bounds wide enough that the lower 5th percentile shows extended failure
decision_rule.RED_overall: No regimen achieves median 80% sustained occupancy at clinically practical dose count; modality cannot be salvaged via repeat dosing; pivot to intra-articular or accept-sub-anakinra-with-cost-edge is needed

il1ra_kd_to_il1r1_nM_prior.anchor: Arend 1990 JCI; Schreuder 1997 Nature; Vigers 1997 Nature crystal structure tier

competitive_antagonism_excess_factor_pri.comment: Arend 1990: 10-100x excess IL-1Ra:IL-1 needed for 50% biological inhibition. Used in supplementary 'biological-inhibition-percent' calculation that translates raw receptor occupancy to functional IL-1 signaling suppression.

regimen_to_explore_n_doses.comment: For each regimen, the dose-count escalation explored. Smallest N where occupancy bar holds is the recommended N for that regimen.
